questions.question: In C, what does *ptr do when ptr is a pointer to int?
options: Dereferences ptr -- accesses the value at the memory address
questions.explanation: * is the dereference operator. ptr holds address; *ptr gets the value there.

questions.question: After calling malloc(), you must:
options: Free the memory when done to avoid leaks
questions.explanation: malloc allocates heap memory. Not freeing causes memory leaks.

questions.question: A segmentation fault most commonly occurs when:
options: You dereference a NULL or invalid pointer
A loop runs too many times
questions.explanation: Dereferencing NULL (int *p=NULL; *p=5) or freed memory causes segfault.

questions.question: The difference between malloc and calloc is:
options: calloc zero-initializes the memory; malloc does not
questions.explanation: calloc(n,size) allocates and zeros all bytes. malloc(n*size) doesn't initialize.

questions.question: In C++, a private member is accessible:
options: By any function in the file
Only by methods within the same class
By any code including the header

questions.question: A pointer in C stores:
options: The memory address of another variable
questions.explanation: int *p = &x; p holds the address of x.

questions.question: sizeof() returns:
options: Size in bytes of a type or variable
questions.explanation: sizeof(int) typically 4 bytes. Essential for portable malloc calls.

questions.question: The -> operator does what?
options: Accesses a struct member through a pointer: ptr->m = (*ptr).m
questions.explanation: Shorthand for (*ptr).member. Used with linked lists and trees.

questions.question: In C++, a constructor is:
options: A special method automatically called when an object is created
questions.explanation: Same name as class, no return type. Called automatically on creation.

questions.question: Stack vs heap memory in C:
options: Stack: auto freed on function exit; Heap: manual malloc/free
questions.explanation: Local variables on stack (auto-freed). Dynamic memory on heap (must free manually).
